title: These 6%+ Dividends Surged 20%+ This Year and It’s Just the Start
author: Michael Foster
description: Two tech-focused closed-end funds (BTX and BST) offer attractive combinations of high dividends (6.1-6.8%), significant price gains (20%+), and wide discounts to net asset value. Despite strong performance this year, both funds trade at discounts well below their historical averages, suggesting further upside potential as market conditions normalize. Both funds are expected to increase payouts given strong NAV gains outpacing current dividend yields.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Top holding in both BTX and BST funds, which have delivered strong performance; represents core AI infrastructure exposure driving fund gains.
Top-3 holding in BST with strong business moats, but article notes it's difficult to argue the stock is 'cheap' given its strong run, suggesting limited near-term upside despite quality.
Top-3 holding in BST with strong competitive moats, but article mentions underwhelming earnings in market news, offsetting the quality positioning.
Top-10 holding in BST, quality large-cap tech company with strong business moats supporting fund performance.

title: This Unstoppable Vanguard ETF Is Obliterating the S&P 500 in 2026, but a Looming Change in Interest Rates Could Halt Its Momentum
author: Anthony Di Pizio
description: The Vanguard Russell 2000 ETF is outperforming the S&P 500 with an 18% year-to-date gain, driven by domestic-focused small-cap companies benefiting from U.S. government policies and AI infrastructure demand. However, rising inflation and anticipated interest rate hikes pose a significant risk, as 32% of Russell 2000 companies carry floating-rate debt that will become more expensive, potentially hampering their growth and earnings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong performance year-to-date (18% gain) and diversification benefits are offset by significant headwinds from anticipated interest rate hikes that disproportionately affect small-cap companies with floating-rate debt.
Stock has surged 1,400% over the last 12 months due to strong demand for clean energy solutions from AI data center operators seeking alternative power sources.
Experiencing blistering demand for energy solutions from the AI industry, driving strong stock returns.
Benefiting from significant demand for energy solutions from the AI industry, resulting in strong stock performance.

Supplying semiconductors and components for AI data centers addressing complex networking and connectivity challenges.

title: These 3 Telecom Stocks Rallied Over 1,000% In A Year: Nvidia Did 75%
author: Piero Cingari
description: Three communications equipment companies—Lumentum Holdings, Applied Optoelectronics, and Ondas Holdings—have each delivered over 1,000% returns in the past year, significantly outpacing Nvidia's 75% gain. Lumentum and Applied Optoelectronics benefit from hyperscaler datacenter buildouts requiring optical components, while Ondas focuses on industrial communications and defense-adjacent autonomy. Wall Street analyst consensus remains mixed, with some targets lagging current prices, particularly for Applied Optoelectronics which shows 35% implied downside.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 1,462% year-over-year return driven by strong demand for optical transceivers in hyperscaler datacenters. Analyst consensus Buy rating with recent upgrades from JP Morgan and Rothschild & Co. Stock trading near analyst targets with minimal downside.

1,164% year-over-year return benefiting from datacenter optical networking demand. However, stock significantly outpaced analyst targets with 35% implied downside, suggesting valuation stretched relative to Wall Street expectations.

1,119% year-over-year return driven by defense-adjacent autonomy and industrial communications demand. Analyst consensus points to 81% upside from current levels, indicating significant conviction despite recent pullback from earlier 2026 highs.

title: Fabrinet Stock Tumbles Despite Q3 Earnings, Revenue Beats
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Fabrinet reported strong Q3 results with earnings per share of $3.72 (beating consensus of $3.56 by 4.49%) and revenue of $1.21 billion (exceeding the $1.18 billion estimate). Despite the earnings beat and record financial results, the stock fell 10.84% to $640 in after-hours trading. The company provided Q4 guidance with adjusted EPS of $3.72-$3.87 and revenue of $1.25-$1.29 billion.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite beating earnings and revenue estimates with record financial results, the stock declined 10.84% in after-hours trading. The negative price action contradicts the positive earnings results, suggesting investor disappointment with either the guidance outlook or other market factors.

title: Meet the Unstoppable Vanguard ETF Beating the S&P 500, the Nasdaq-100, and the Dow Jones in 2026
author: Anthony Di Pizio
description: The Vanguard Russell 2000 ETF is outperforming major U.S. indexes in 2026 with an 11.1% return year-to-date. Small-cap companies in the Russell 2000 are more insulated from geopolitical risks as they conduct most business domestically, while large-cap tech-heavy indexes face headwinds from global instability. The Russell's diversified sector composition and exposure to beneficiaries of U.S. government policies position it to continue outperforming in 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The ETF is outperforming major indexes with 11.1% YTD returns in 2026. Its diversified portfolio, domestic focus, and insulation from geopolitical risks position it favorably for continued outperformance.
Stock has surged 1,100% over the past year, benefiting from explosive demand for clean energy solutions from AI data center operators and U.S. manufacturing advantages.
